headline: Southwest Airlines’ Move to Give Up Fuel Hedging Program Is Proving Costly As Oil Surges
summary: Southwest Airlines  picked the wrong time to give up its fuel hedging program.  Delta Air Lines  owns a U.S. refinery that can offer some relief from higher fuel costs.  The Southwest program likely would have provided large benefits to the company, since jet-fuel prices have doubled to about $4.25 a gallon in 2026, according to OPIS, an energy information company owned by Dow Jones, the publisher of Barron’s.

raw.headline: Southwest Airlines’ Move to Give Up Fuel Hedging Program Is Proving Costly As Oil Surges
raw.summary: Southwest Airlines  picked the wrong time to give up its fuel hedging program.  Delta Air Lines  owns a U.S. refinery that can offer some relief from higher fuel costs. headline: Delta Air Lines scraps secret perk for lawmakers amid shutdown
summary: As many airports across the U.S. continue to see brutally long lines of travelers waiting to clear TSA amid the extended government shutdown, the Senate unanimously passed a proposal to suspend the preferential treatment that allows members of Congress to bypass regular screening at airports. ...

headline: Delta suspends 'specialty services' perk for members of Congress, cites DHS shutdown
summary: Delta Air Lines' move comes as airports around the U.S. are seeing extra-long security lines as a result of elevated absences by TSA agents.

headline: Travel Chaos Across U.S. as LaGuardia Airport Faces More Disruption After Fatal Crash
summary: New York’s LaGuardia Airport was still experiencing flight cancellations early Tuesday after a fatal runway collision, while travelers continued to face hourslong wait times at a number of airports.

headline: United ditches more economy seats to make room for bigger premium cabins with new layouts
summary: United will have new cabins on smaller planes with premium seats, including those that convert into a bed, to win more sales from high-spending travelers.

headline: Trump Blinks on Iran And Wall Street Thanks Him for It
summary: A five-day pause on potential U.S. strikes against Iranian energy infrastructure was all it took to flip a sea of red into a splash of green on Monday. Oil fell hard, rates retreated, and cruise and airline stocks surged, while Tesla's Terafab chip ambitions and Berkshire's latest Japan move added some genuine corporate substance to the session.

raw.headline: Trump Blinks on Iran And Wall Street Thanks Him for It
